zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de Ludlow I於1265年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾盧德洛福 卡素時，他的父親Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow26歲，母親Agnes Audley8歲。他於1299年之前與Joane II De Marmion結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1314年在英國林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ludlow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from the Old English river name Hlūde (from hlūd ‘loud, roaring’) referring to the Teme river + hlāw ‘hill’. This name was taken Ireland in the 13th and 17th centuries. See also Laidlaw .

variants.name: Laidlaw
meanings: Scottish: Border surname found mainly around Selkirk and the vales of Ettrick and Yarrow; a Scottish development of   Ludlow  .

variants.name: Ledlow
meanings: English, Irish, or Scottish: variant of   Ludlow   or   Laidlaw  .

variants.name: Ludlam
meanings: English: possibly from a misreading of w as m in Ludelawe, a recorded medieval spelling of the placename Ludlow in Shropshire. Compare Ludlow .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de Ludlow I was born in 1265, in Ludlow Castle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow, was 26 and his mother, Agnes Audley, was 8. He married Joane II De Marmion before 1299. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1314, in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 49.

Il a épousé Joane II De Marmion avant 1299. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1314, à Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Ludlow I(이)가 1265년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 루들로 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Audley(은)는 8세였다. 그는 1299년 이전 Joane II De Marmion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1314년 영국 링코실르 스크라이벨스비에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.
